removed.evolving wilds: [[Evolving Wilds]] was outclassed by more efficient fetch lands.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s average mana value by replacing expensive draw spells, top-end planeswalkers, and slower ramp with cheaper interaction, efficient proliferate, and faster fixing. The added fetches, triomes, and two-mana rocks make four colors smoother without leaning on clunkier three-mana mana sources. It also trims cards outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]'s color identity, keeping the deck cleaner and more consistent at casual power.

The added suite broadens coverage across spells and permanents, while the cuts trim slower card-draw pieces, a color-identity problem card, and a few lands to make room. Overall, the deck becomes more responsive at casual tables without changing its core game plan.

added.arcane denial: [[Arcane Denial]] adds cheap stack interaction to protect your board or stop key opposing plays.
added.assassin's trophy: [[Assassin's Trophy]] gives flexible instant-speed removal for nearly any problematic permanent.
added.beast within: [[Beast Within]] answers any permanent type, which improves the deck's interaction spread.
added.command beacon: [[Command Beacon]] helps recover [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] after repeated commander tax.
added.dovin's veto: [[Dovin's Veto]] is efficient protection against noncreature spells and cannot be countered.
added.generous gift: [[Generous Gift]] provides broad permanent removal at instant speed.
added.swan song: [[Swan Song]] is a very cheap way to stop key enchantments, instants, and sorceries.
added.utter end: [[Utter End]] offers clean exile-based removal for troublesome nonland permanents.
added.vindicate: [[Vindicate]] adds versatile sorcery-speed removal that can hit almost anything.

removed.swamp: A second [[Swamp]] was cut to make room while keeping the mana base intact.
removed.blue sun's zenith: [[Blue Sun's Zenith]] is mana-hungry and slower than the interaction this pass prioritizes.
removed.consecrated sphinx: [[Consecrated Sphinx]] is a powerful draw engine, but it is less aligned with this interaction-focused pass.
removed.forest: A [[Forest]] was trimmed to help fit the new spell package.
removed.island: An [[Island]] was removed as part of making room for more answers.
removed.nicol bolas, dragon-god: [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] falls outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]'s color identity and needed to be replaced.
removed.pull from tomorrow: [[Pull from Tomorrow]] was cut because the pass prefers cheaper, more immediate interaction over big draw spells.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] is less important than adding a utility land that supports commander access.
removed.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] is another expensive draw spell that gives way to broader interaction.

results.why: This budget pass replaces many premium staples and expensive lands with lower-cost alternatives that still support [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] through proliferate, planeswalkers, and steady fixing. The changes keep the deck casual and functional while trimming high-dollar interaction, top-end threats, and mana base costs. Several swaps also help clean up color concerns by replacing off-identity or overly expensive options with safer, budget-friendly inclusions.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck's pressure and removes some of the swingiest or more competitive effects while keeping the [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] proliferate theme intact. The additions lean into fair interaction, poison/proliferate synergy, and big splashy value plays that fit a more casual table. The cuts trim high-end planeswalker combos, oppressive walkers, and oversized instant-speed card draw to soften the deck's play patterns.

The additions lean into fair interaction, poison/proliferate synergy, and big splashy value plays that fit a more casual table. added.beast within: [[Beast Within]] adds flexible, fair interaction that answers almost any permanent.
added.contentious plan: [[Contentious Plan]] is a simple proliferate spell that supports Atraxa without spiking power too hard.
added.drown in ichor: [[Drown in Ichor]] gives creature removal while adding a poison-themed proliferate piece.
added.generous gift: [[Generous Gift]] is broad removal that fits a casual interactive game plan.
added.grateful apparition: [[Grateful Apparition]] provides repeatable proliferate through combat in a slower, more interactive way.
added.ichormoon gauntlet: [[Ichormoon Gauntlet]] adds proliferate value for a planeswalker shell without relying on combo lines.
added.merciless eviction: [[Merciless Eviction]] is a flexible reset button that helps answer varied casual boards.
added.planewide celebration: [[Planewide Celebration]] is a big splashy payoff that creates value through multiple proliferate choices.
added.teferi's protection: [[Teferi's Protection]] protects your board and life total without advancing oppressive lock pieces.

removed.blue sun's zenith: [[Blue Sun's Zenith]] is a large instant-speed draw spell that pushes the deck toward stronger late-game bursts.
removed.karn liberated: [[Karn Liberated]] is a highly threatening planeswalker that can dominate casual tables on its own.
removed.narset, parter of veils: [[Narset, Parter of Veils]] creates frustrating play patterns by shutting down extra card draw.
removed.nicol bolas, dragon-god: [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] is outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]'s color identity and had to be removed.
removed.oko, thief of crowns: [[Oko, Thief of Crowns]] is an unusually efficient planeswalker that can overpower casual games.
removed.pull from tomorrow: [[Pull from Tomorrow]] is another high-volume draw spell that is less thematic than the new proliferate pieces.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] is a lower-synergy utility land that makes room for more on-theme spells.
removed.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] adds another scalable draw burst that is stronger than needed for this pass.
removed.tezzeret the seeker: [[Tezzeret the Seeker]] can tutor key artifacts too efficiently for a more casual build.
removed.the chain veil: [[The Chain Veil]] supports powerful planeswalker combo turns that this casual pass is intentionally trimming.

The adds lean into cheap token payoffs, efficient ramp, and smoother mana, while the cuts remove expensive top-end, slower token makers, and clunkier lands. The result is a faster casual build that still supports the deck's populate plan.

added.aura shards: [[Aura Shards]] turns your token production into repeatable artifact and enchantment removal.
added.beast within: [[Beast Within]] is a flexible answer to nearly any permanent.

added.boros signet: [[Boros Signet]] improves fixing and helps deploy interaction on time.
added.chaos warp: [[Chaos Warp]] answers problematic permanents that Naya colors can otherwise struggle with.
added.forest: This [[Forest]] helps support the updated mana needs after the swap.
added.krosan grip: [[Krosan Grip]] cleanly removes key artifacts or enchantments through interaction.
added.return to dust: [[Return to Dust]] offers efficient exile-based removal for artifacts and enchantments.
added.stroke of midnight: [[Stroke of Midnight]] is broad instant-speed interaction for any nonland permanent.
added.wear // tear: [[Wear // Tear]] adds cheap and flexible artifact or enchantment removal.
added.winds of abandon: [[Winds of Abandon]] doubles as spot removal or a one-sided board clear.

removed.eyes in the skies: [[Eyes in the Skies]] is a low-impact token maker compared to the new interaction.
removed.godsire: [[Godsire]] is powerful but very expensive and slow for this pass.
removed.grove of the guardian: [[Grove of the Guardian]] is mana-intensive and slower than a basic land here.
removed.growing ranks: [[Growing Ranks]] is a slow payoff that does not interact with opposing threats.
removed.mountain: This [[Mountain]] was cut to make room for a greener mana source.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] is utility the deck can trim for stronger interaction and stability.
removed.selesnya guildmage: [[Selesnya Guildmage]] is a mana-hungry token outlet that is less efficient here.
removed.trostani's summoner: [[Trostani's Summoner]] is a slow top-end token card that does not answer problems.
removed.utvara hellkite: [[Utvara Hellkite]] is a costly finisher that is outside the pass focus.
removed.vitu-ghazi guildmage: [[Vitu-Ghazi Guildmage]] is another slow mana sink trimmed for more direct answers.

added.island: This [[Island]] helps rebalance the mana after the land changes.
added.moon-circuit hacker: [[Moon-Circuit Hacker]] is an on-theme ninja that provides modest, interactive card advantage.

added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] is efficient interaction that answers problematic creatures cleanly.
added.reconnaissance mission: [[Reconnaissance Mission]] rewards combat damage with steady card flow instead of huge spikes.

added.temple of the false god: [[Temple of the False God]] adds a slower ramp-oriented land suited to a softer build.

raw.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.95.
raw.plan: This pass makes [[Teysa Karlov]] play in a more casual, board-focused way by swapping some of the deck’s strongest mana and card-advantage pieces for fairer creature-based engines and simple sacrifice payoffs. The added cards reward tokens, deaths, and small creatures without pushing the deck toward explosive combo turns. Trimming extra lands and a few high-power staples also helps the list feel more interactive and less oppressive at a casual table.
raw.why: This pass makes [[Teysa Karlov]] play in a more casual, board-focused way by swapping some of the deck’s strongest mana and card-advantage pieces for fairer creature-based engines and simple sacrifice payoffs. The added cards reward tokens, deaths, and small creatures without pushing the deck toward explosive combo turns. Trimming extra lands and a few high-power staples also helps the list feel more interactive and less oppressive at a casual table.

added.bygone bishop: [[Bygone Bishop]] adds steady value from casting small creatures in a slower, fairer way.
added.deadly dispute: [[Deadly Dispute]] gives the deck a simple, efficient sacrifice outlet that replaces itself.
added.grim haruspex: [[Grim Haruspex]] turns creature deaths into card draw that fits the deck’s core theme.
added.hunted witness: [[Hunted Witness]] is a low-pressure fodder creature that leaves behind another body when it dies.
added.living death: [[Living Death]] creates a big, swingy graveyard moment that suits a casual aristocrats deck.
added.mentor of the meek: [[Mentor of the Meek]] rewards the deck for making lots of small creatures.
added.midnight reaper: [[Midnight Reaper]] provides more death-triggered card draw on a fair creature body.
added.ministrant of obligation: [[Ministrant of Obligation]] supplies multiple bodies from one card for sacrifice and death triggers.
added.morbid opportunist: [[Morbid Opportunist]] offers controlled, once-per-turn card draw from creatures dying.
added.orzhov enforcer: [[Orzhov Enforcer]] is an early deathtouch creature that also leaves behind value when it dies.
added.selfless spirit: [[Selfless Spirit]] adds light protection while still working with sacrifice and death synergies.
added.village rites: [[Village Rites]] is a clean, low-cost way to cash in a creature for cards.
added.welcoming vampire: [[Welcoming Vampire]] gives repeatable card draw from the deck’s small token and creature entries.

removed.plains: Extra [[Plains]] were cut to lower the land count and make room for more action spells.
removed.swamp: Extra [[Swamp]] were cut to lower the land count and make room for more action spells.
removed.castle ardenvale: [[Castle Ardenvale]] was trimmed as part of simplifying the mana base for a more casual build.
removed.castle locthwain: [[Castle Locthwain]] was removed to reduce pushed repeatable card advantage from the mana base.
removed.dark confidant: [[Dark Confidant]] is a strong, efficient draw engine that is less casual than the new options.
removed.farewell: [[Farewell]] is a very sweeping reset that can feel harsh in lower-powered games.
removed.grave pact: [[Grave Pact]] often locks down creature decks too hard for a casual table.
removed.high market: [[High Market]] was cut to reduce the density of free sacrifice outlets on lands.
removed.necropotence: [[Necropotence]] is an exceptionally powerful draw engine that pushes the deck above casual pacing.
removed.phyrexian altar: [[Phyrexian Altar]] enables explosive sacrifice loops that are stronger than this pass aims for.
removed.phyrexian tower: [[Phyrexian Tower]] was removed to tone down fast mana from sacrifice synergies.
removed.spectator seating: [[Spectator Seating]] was removed because it falls outside [[Teysa Karlov]]'s color identity.

It also cleans up some clunkier interaction and replaces off-plan or color-identity-problematic slots with cards that better support death triggers and token play.

raw.plan: This pass trims slower, lower-synergy cards and excess basics to tighten [[Teysa Karlov]]'s aristocrats engine at a casual-optimized level. The additions improve cheap card flow, tutoring, sacrifice outlets, and recursion so the deck can assemble value more consistently. It also cleans up some clunkier interaction and replaces off-plan or color-identity-problematic slots with cards that better support death triggers and token play.
raw.why: This pass trims slower, lower-synergy cards and excess basics to tighten [[Teysa Karlov]]'s aristocrats engine at a casual-optimized level. The additions improve cheap card flow, tutoring, sacrifice outlets, and recursion so the deck can assemble value more consistently. It also cleans up some clunkier interaction and replaces off-plan or color-identity-problematic slots with cards that better support death triggers and token play.

added.boromir, warden of the tower: [[Boromir, Warden of the Tower]] adds protection and leaves a body behind for sacrifice lines.
added.deadly dispute: [[Deadly Dispute]] is an efficient sacrifice outlet that converts fodder into cards and mana.
added.demonic tutor: [[Demonic Tutor]] finds the best payoff or missing engine piece with minimal setup.
added.diabolic intent: [[Diabolic Intent]] turns expendable creatures into a cheap tutor for key synergies.
added.eiganjo, seat of the empire: [[Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire]] upgrades a land slot into flexible interaction.
added.esper sentinel: [[Esper Sentinel]] improves early card flow at a very low cost.
added.grim haruspex: [[Grim Haruspex]] rewards your sacrifice plan with repeatable card draw.
added.living death: [[Living Death]] can swing games by rebuilding your board while retriggering death synergies.
added.lurrus of the dream-den: [[Lurrus of the Dream-Den]] recurs many cheap permanents that naturally get traded off.
added.midnight reaper: [[Midnight Reaper]] adds more death-trigger card draw to keep resources flowing.
added.morbid opportunist: [[Morbid Opportunist]] provides steady card advantage from creatures dying each turn.
added.recruiter of the guard: [[Recruiter of the Guard]] helps find small utility creatures that support the engine.
added.rite of oblivion: [[Rite of Oblivion]] gives efficient removal while using disposable tokens well.
added.smothering tithe: [[Smothering Tithe]] boosts mana production so the deck can double-spell more easily.
added.takenuma, abandoned mire: [[Takenuma, Abandoned Mire]] adds low-cost recursion from a land slot.
added.unearth: [[Unearth]] cheaply returns many important creatures after trades or sacrifice loops.
added.victimize: [[Victimize]] turns one expendable creature into a strong reanimation play.
added.village rites: [[Village Rites]] is a cheap way to cash in fodder for cards.
added.welcoming vampire: [[Welcoming Vampire]] draws cards naturally off the deck's many small creatures and tokens.
added.woe strider: [[Woe Strider]] provides a free sacrifice outlet plus recurring value.

removed.swamp: Extra [[Swamp]] copies were trimmed to make room for stronger nonland upgrades.
removed.plains: Extra [[Plains]] copies were trimmed to open slots for more impactful spells.
removed.dark confidant: [[Dark Confidant]] is less reliable here than draw tied directly to creature deaths.
removed.emeria shepherd: [[Emeria Shepherd]] is powerful but expensive and slower than the deck wants.
removed.evolving wilds: [[Evolving Wilds]] was cut for a faster, cleaner mana base.
removed.farewell: [[Farewell]] clears your own graveyard and board synergies too harshly.
removed.finale of glory: [[Finale of Glory]] is mana-hungry compared to lower-cost token and value options.
removed.high market: [[High Market]] is a weaker sacrifice outlet than the new spell and creature options.
removed.merciless eviction: [[Merciless Eviction]] is broad but expensive and often anti-synergistic with your board.
removed.night's whisper: [[Night's Whisper]] was replaced by draw that better synergizes with sacrificing creatures.
removed.painful truths: [[Painful Truths]] is less efficient here and does not advance the sacrifice plan.
removed.phyrexian arena: [[Phyrexian Arena]] is slower than the deck's new burst and death-based draw.
removed.phyrexian tower: [[Phyrexian Tower]] was removed during trimming despite its synergy to keep the list legal and streamlined.
removed.read the bones: [[Read the Bones]] is slower than newer card-draw options added in this pass.
removed.scoured barrens: [[Scoured Barrens]] was cut to reduce tapped lands and improve speed.
removed.secure the wastes: [[Secure the Wastes]] is flexible but less efficient than the new engine pieces.
removed.sign in blood: [[Sign in Blood]] was replaced by card draw that better uses sacrifice fodder.
removed.spawning pit: [[Spawning Pit]] is a slower outlet than the upgraded sacrifice package.
removed.spectator seating: [[Spectator Seating]] falls outside [[Teysa Karlov]]'s color identity.
removed.spectral procession: [[Spectral Procession]] is awkward on mana and less efficient than other token support.
removed.sram's expertise: [[Sram's Expertise]] is solid value but weaker than the tighter additions.
removed.terramorphic expanse: [[Terramorphic Expanse]] was cut for a faster mana base and higher card quality.
removed.utter end: [[Utter End]] is flexible but inefficient next to cheaper interaction like [[Rite of Oblivion]].
